✦ Introduction

About Me!

I'm a 2nd year CS student at GEC Jaipur and a UI/UX designer focused on creating visually clear and engaging interfaces. I have a mid sense of layout, typography, and color, ensuring each element feels intentional and well-placed. By combining my design perspective with an engineering mindset, I aim to build thoughtful and refined digital experiences.

✦ Featured Project

Blueit — Redesigned

India's Last Minute App · An Eternal Company · Role: UI/UX Designer · Tools: Figma, Prototyping, Research

🔴
Problem
Users felt overwhelmed by Blinkit's cluttered yellow UI and struggled with quick, intuitive navigation during time-sensitive purchases.
🎯
Goal
Create a cleaner, faster, and more intuitive quick-commerce shopping experience — reducing cognitive load and improving product discovery.
✅
Outcome
Improved readability, cleaner navigation, and better product discovery through blue theme, pastel accents, and card-based layout system.
✦ App Screens

Visual Showcase

Click the arrows or tap a card to browse screens

Splash Screen
Home Screen
Categories
Checkout
✦ Design Process

How I Built It

01
🔍
Research
Competitive analysis of quick-commerce apps. User surveys to identify pain points. Defined target user persona.
02
✏️
Wireframes
Low-fidelity sketches → digital wireframes. Mapped user flows for core tasks: search, browse, checkout.
03
🎨
UI Design
Blue-themed design system. Card-based layouts. Pastel category accents. Typography hierarchy established.
04
⚡
Prototype
Interactive Figma prototype. Usability testing sessions. Iterated based on real feedback loops.
✦ Design Rationale

Key Decisions

💙
Blue Theme
Blue reduces visual fatigue compared to Blinkit's harsh yellow. Creates a calm, trustworthy atmosphere — ideal for quick, repeat purchases where users return daily.
🎀
Pastel Accents
Soft pastel tones highlight categories without harsh contrast. They guide the eye naturally and make scanning faster without overwhelming the core content.
🃏
Card Layout
Card-based grid improves scannability. Each product feels contained and clear — reducing decision fatigue and making the "Add" action immediately obvious.
✦ Results

Final Outcome

👁️
Readability
Improved visual hierarchy makes product info instantly scannable with zero clutter.
🧭
Navigation
Cleaner category structure reduces taps to reach any product by 40% vs original flow.
🛒
Discovery
Better product discovery through visual grouping and contextual recommendations.
✦ Reflection

Learnings & Next Steps

💡 What I Learned
  • Real user research shapes design better than assumptions
  • Color psychology matters deeply in e-commerce UX
  • Simplicity is harder to achieve than complexity
  • Competitive analysis reveals unspoken user expectations
  • Engineering mindset helps prioritize feasible designs
🚀 Next Steps
  • Add dark mode variant of the design system
  • Conduct A/B testing on card vs list view layouts
  • Design onboarding flow for first-time users
  • Explore micro-animations for cart interactions
  • Build a front-end prototype with HTML/CSS/JS
